name: Stewed Mushrooms & Queens
recipeIngredient: 1/4 pint Single Cream or Jersey Milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the mushrooms thickly.
2. Place them in a pan with cold milk (and cream, if using). Cover three-quarters of the pan and bring to a fast simmer.
3. Add the bay leaf and a pinch of salt, then reduce heat to very low. Cover three-quarters again and stew gently for 5-6 minutes.
4. Strain the liquid from the mushrooms, discard the bay leaf, and keep the mushrooms warm in a very low oven.
5. Prepare a smooth sauce by combining butter, flour, and the mushroom cooking liquor. Stir in at least half a teaspoon of tarragon vinegar and anchovy essence. Let it simmer for 2-3 minutes before seasoning with salt and pepper.
6. Quickly stir in the bay scallops (queens) and immediately reduce heat to low. Cover three-quarters of the pan, stirring occasionally until only a very occasional bubble appears.
7. Remove from the heat, then add the strained mushroom liquid and season again if needed. Transfer the contents into a warmed gratin dish or divided scallop shells for serving.
8. Garnish with fresh parsley and fried breadcrumbs or triangles of fried bread. Serve as an appetizer, or pair with green beans and rice for a main course.
Serves 2 as an appetizer, 3 as a main course, or 6 as part of a larger meal.
